provenance.source_corpus: Recovery in a multiple stressor environment: using the reference condition approach to examine zooplankton community change along opposing gradients

nodes.name: large reductions in SO 2 and metal emissions

source_spans.text: With the implementation of large reductions in SO 2 and metal emissions, water quality has improved
With the implementation of large reductions in SO 2 and metal emissions, water quality has improved
While increases in pH have led to an overall recovery of zooplankton
While increases in pH have led to an overall recovery of zooplankton
In this region, a pH of 6 has been used as a threshold at which signiﬁcant biological damage is known to occur
In this region, a pH of 6 has been used as a threshold at which signiﬁcant biological damage is known to occur
substantial community recovery was evident in lakes with pH .5.5
substantial community recovery was evident in lakes with pH .5.5
most lakes with pH ,5.5 contained species poor copepod communities
most lakes with pH ,5.5 contained species poor copepod communities
Zooplankton communities in many of the urban lakes were found to be impaired despite years of non-acidic conditions, which may be attributable to elevated metal concentrations
Zooplankton communities in many of the urban lakes were found to be impaired despite years of non-acidic conditions, which may be attributable to elevated metal concentrations
Copepod recovery may have been limited by residual metal toxicity
Copepod recovery may have been limited by residual metal toxicity
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity , which appears to exclude both piscivorous ﬁsh and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity , which appears to exclude both piscivorous ﬁsh and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity , which appears to exclude both piscivorous ﬁsh and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
changes in nutrient concentrations resulting from urbanization and alterations in ﬁsh community structure are contributing to the lack of recovery in some of the urban lakes
changes in nutrient concentrations resulting from urbanization and alterations in ﬁsh community structure are contributing to the lack of recovery in some of the urban lakes
zooplankton communities do recover when lake pH rises above 6
zooplankton communities do recover when lake pH rises above 6
low concentrations of DOC and ions, which can increase metal toxicity through increased bioavailability
low concentrations of DOC and ions, which can increase metal toxicity through increased bioavailability
The most important variable was alkalinity , which alone explained 15% of the variation
The most important variable was alkalinity , which alone explained 15% of the variation
Cyclopoid copepods such as D. b. thomasi and M. edax were strongly , negatively correlated with pH

nodes.name: abundance of D. b. thomasi and M. edax

source_spans.text: Cyclopoid copepods such as D. b. thomasi and M. edax were strongly , negatively correlated with pH
distance from reference condition appeared to increase for copepods ( r ¼ 0.35, P ¼ 0.07)
distance from reference condition appeared to increase for copepods ( r ¼ 0.35, P ¼ 0.07)
Johnny Lake, a ﬁshless lake, was found to be signiﬁcantly impaired for both the cladoceran and copepod community
